vitals.date: No confirmed edition since 2019 -- 2020 cancelled (extreme weather), 2021 cancelled (COVID-19); no evidence of revival through 2026
vitals.field_size: 3,000+ entries (2020 edition, before same-morning cancellation); no verified figure since

course_description.character: A real Western Cape winelands road race with real history -- but no confirmed edition since 2019, and not listed on Pedal Power Association's current 2026 road calendar. It ran for years as Die Burger Cycle Tour (through at least 2013, based at Stellenbosch High School, roughly 92 km), was renamed Stellenbosch Cycle Tour in 2014 (roughly 95 km; organizers projected around 7,500 riders pre-race, not a confirmed turnout figure), and became the Medihelp Stellenbosch Cycle Tour from 2016 (typically 97 km, 102 km for its 2019 edition from Val de Vie Estate). It functioned as a seeding race for the much larger Cape Town Cycle Tour, run under the same Cape Town Cycle Tour Trust / Pedal Power Association organizational ecosystem -- but it is a separate, distinct event in a different city, not a mislabeled or duplicated Cape Town Cycle Tour entry. The scheduled January 2020 edition, back at Stellenbosch High School, was cancelled on race morning after winds along the route were forecast to reach approximately 95 km/h. The 2021 edition was cancelled outright due to COVID-19. No edition has appeared on Pedal Power Association's road calendars since, and the event's former official domain (stellenboschcycletour.co.za) currently resolves to a parked-domain placeholder page.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Abundant wine estates, hotels, and guesthouses in Stellenbosch winelands.

history.origin_story: Descended from Die Burger Cycle Tour (based at Stellenbosch High School), which was rebranded the Stellenbosch Cycle Tour in 2014 and later sponsored as the Medihelp Stellenbosch Cycle Tour from 2016. Functioned as a premier Cape Town Cycle Tour seeding race for the Western Cape winelands. Its last completed edition ran in 2019; the January 2020 edition was cancelled the morning of due to extreme wind forecasts, and 2021 was cancelled for COVID-19. No edition has surfaced since.

biased_opinion.summary: There is no current Stellenbosch Cycle Tour to rate. This was a real, mass-participation Western Cape road race -- not, as is sometimes assumed, a mislabeled duplicate of the Cape Town Cycle Tour, though the two shared organizational roots and the Stellenbosch race served as a seeding event for its much larger Cape Town cousin. Its last completed edition ran in 2019 (102 km, from Val de Vie Estate). The scheduled January 2020 edition was cancelled on race morning after winds along the route were forecast to reach roughly 95 km/h; more than 3,000 riders had entered. The 2021 edition was cancelled outright for COVID-19. Since then, it hasn't appeared on Pedal Power Association's road calendars through 2026, and the event's own former domain now resolves to a parked-domain placeholder. There has been no formal announcement that the event is permanently over -- this profile calls it 'defunct' as the closest fit within this database's status options, not as confirmation of a formal closure.
weaknesses: This event does not currently exist -- it should not be presented to riders as a bookable 2026 race
The course and distance changed across eras (Stellenbosch High School vs. Val de Vie Estate; 92 to 102 km depending on the year) -- there's no single fixed course to describe
The 2020 edition was cancelled on race morning due to extreme weather, and 2021 was cancelled outright for COVID-19, with no revival evidence since -- a much longer gap than a normal one-year hiatus
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Retired, or at minimum indefinitely dormant. If you want a Western Cape winelands road event as Cape Town Cycle Tour prep, the still-active Rhodes Quality Winelands Cycle Race (Wellington) is the closest real current equivalent -- a separate event, not a renamed successor.

final_verdict.one_liner: A once-real Western Cape winelands road tour, now dormant with no confirmed edition since 2019.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No -- this event does not currently exist. For a Cape Winelands road tour as Cape Town Cycle Tour prep, look at the Rhodes Quality Winelands Cycle Race (Wellington) instead.
final_verdict.alternatives: Rhodes Quality Winelands Cycle Race (Wellington, ~102km/52km, active as of 2026) or the Cape Town Cycle Tour itself (Cape Town, a much larger, entirely separate event).

catalog_flags.status_note: Confirmed a real, historically distinct event from the Cape Town Cycle Tour -- not a geocoding or duplicate-name error (they share an organizational lineage but are separate races in different cities). However, the event appears dormant/defunct: last completed edition 2019, 2020 cancelled morning-of for extreme weather, 2021 cancelled for COVID-19, and no edition has surfaced anywhere (organizer calendars, entry platforms, media) through 2026. Flagging for a human call on whether this profile should remain in the active catalog as a dormant/defunct entry (per the birkebeinerrittet-road precedent) or be removed.

eligibility.notes: No evidence of any Stellenbosch Cycle Tour edition since 2019. The January 2020 edition was cancelled race-morning due to forecast 95 km/h winds (3,000+ entries); the following edition was cancelled for COVID-19 per the Pedal Power Association's own 2021 report. The organizer's current 2026 road-event calendar does not include it, and its former official domain (stellenboschcycletour.co.za) currently resolves to a parked-domain page. 'Defunct' is used here as the closest fit within this database's fixed status options (active/defunct/cancelled/unknown) -- there is no formal organizer announcement of permanent closure, just a 5+ year absence with no revival evidence. This is a real, historically distinct event from the much larger Cape Town Cycle Tour, not a mislabeled duplicate of it.
